---
title: "ICRG Poster Plotting"
author: "Cooper Joseph Sharp"
date: "2025-08-12"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

```

```{r}

# Load packages
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidycensus)

```

```{r}

# Load dataset
df <- read.csv("../../derivatives/scoring/scoring-mastersheet.csv")

```

```{r}
# Remove NAs and "Prefer not to respond", recode minority status, and compute counts + proportions
df_race <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(demo_race), demo_race != 7) %>%
  mutate(minority_status = case_when(
    demo_race == 5 ~ "Non-Minority",
    TRUE ~ "Minority"
  )) %>%
  group_by(minority_status) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    Proportion = Count / sum(Count),
    minority_status = factor(minority_status, levels = c("Non-Minority", "Minority"))
  )

# Bar plot: Count on y-axis, % labels above bars
plot_race <- ggplot(df_race, aes(x = minority_status, y = Count, fill = minority_status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = scales::percent(Proportion, accuracy = 1)),
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 8,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +  # Dynamic y-axis with space above bars
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Count") +
  ylim(0, 850) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#AFCBB4", "#8E9A84")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90"),

    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),

    # Legend styling
    legend.position = c(0.95, 0.95),
    legend.justification = c("right", "top"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white"),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 24, color = "black"),
    legend.key.size = unit(1.2, "cm"),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.3, "cm")
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(byrow = TRUE))

print(plot_race)



```

```{r}

# Prepare gender count and proportion data
df_gender <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(demo_gender), demo_gender %in% c(1, 2)) %>%
  mutate(gender_label = case_when(
    demo_gender == 1 ~ "Male",
    demo_gender == 2 ~ "Female"
  )) %>%
  group_by(gender_label) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    Proportion = Count / sum(Count),
    gender_label = factor(gender_label, levels = c("Male", "Female"))
  )

# Create bar plot with count on y-axis and percent above bars
plot_gender <- ggplot(df_gender, aes(x = gender_label, y = Count, fill = gender_label)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = scales::percent(Proportion, accuracy = 1)),
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 8,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +  # gives headroom for labels
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Count") +
  ylim(0, 600) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#AFCBB4", "#8E9A84")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90"),

    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),

    # Legend styling
    legend.position = c(0.95, 0.95),
    legend.justification = c("right", "top"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white"),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 24, color = "black"),
    legend.key.size = unit(1.2, "cm"),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.3, "cm")
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(byrow = TRUE))

print(plot_gender)


```

```{r}

## Not currently being used in poster. 

# Recalculate df_prob to keep both Count and Proportion
df_prob <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(prob_gam), prob_gam %in% c("yes", "no")) %>%
  mutate(gambling_status = case_when(
    prob_gam == "yes" ~ "Problem Gamblers",
    prob_gam == "no" ~ "No Problem Gambling"
  )) %>%
  group_by(gambling_status) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    Proportion = Count / sum(Count),
    gambling_status = factor(gambling_status, levels = c("No Problem Gambling", "Problem Gamblers"))
  )

# Create bar plot with count on y-axis and percentage labels
plot_gam <- ggplot(df_prob, aes(x = gambling_status, y = Count, fill = gambling_status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = scales::percent(Proportion, accuracy = 1)),
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 8,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +  # dynamic limit with a bit of headroom
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Count") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("#AFCBB4", "#8E9A84"),
    labels = c("Non-PG", "PG")
  ) +
  ylim(0, 800) + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    legend.position = "none"

)
print(plot_gam)


```

```{r}

library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)

# Remove NAs
df <- df[!is.na(df$demo_yrs), ]

# Descriptive statistics
mean_age <- round(mean(df$demo_yrs), 2)
sd_age <- round(sd(df$demo_yrs), 2)

# Plot
plot_density_simple <- ggplot(df, aes(x = demo_yrs)) +
  geom_density(fill = "#8E8577", alpha = 0.5, color = "black", size = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Density") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(18, 80), breaks = c(18, 80)) +
  ylim(0, 0.035) +

theme_minimal(base_size = 24) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),  # Remove x tick marks
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold")
  )

print(plot_density_simple)


```
```{r}

plot_race <- plot_race + theme(legend.position = "none")
plot_gender <- plot_gender + theme(legend.position = "none")
plot_gam <- plot_gam + theme(legend.position = "none")
plot_density_simple <- plot_density_simple + theme(legend.position = "none")

```


```{r}

ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_race.svg", plot_race, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 300)
ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_gender.svg", plot_gender, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 300)
ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_gam.svg", plot_gam, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 300)
ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_age.svg", plot_density_simple, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 300)

```

```{r}

# Composite BIS/BAS score
df$bisbas_bas <- df$bisbas_drive + df$bisbas_fun_seek + df$bisbas_reward_res

# Calculate z-scores
calculate_z <- function(X, X_mean, S) {
  return((X - X_mean) / S)
}

df$bisbas_bas_z_score <- calculate_z(
  df$bisbas_bas,
  mean(df$bisbas_bas, na.rm = TRUE),
  sd(df$bisbas_bas, na.rm = TRUE))

df$spsrq_sr_z_score <- calculate_z(
  df$spsrq_sr,
  mean(df$spsrq_sr, na.rm = TRUE),
  sd(df$spsrq_sr, na.rm = TRUE))

# Relative sensitivity: BISBAS - SPSRQ
df$rs_standard <- df$bisbas_bas_z_score - df$spsrq_sr_z_score

# Remove outliers in rs_standard
q3 <- quantile(df$rs_standard, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
q1 <- quantile(df$rs_standard, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
iqr <- q3 - q1
upper_bound <- q3 + 1.5 * iqr
lower_bound <- q1 - 1.5 * iqr
df <- df[!(df$rs_standard >= upper_bound | df$rs_standard <= lower_bound) | is.na(df$rs_standard), ]

# Composite reward score
df$rew_composite <- df$spsrq_sr_z_score + df$bisbas_bas_z_score

```

```{r}

# === Label mappings ===
income_labels <- c(
  "1" = "$0 to $9,999", "2" = "$10,000 to $14,999", "3" = "$15,000 to $19,999", "4" = "$20,000 to $24,999",
  "5" = "$25,000 to $29,999", "6" = "$30,000 to $34,999", "7" = "$35,000 to $39,999", "8" = "$40,000 to $44,999",
  "9" = "$45,000 to $49,999", "10" = "$50,000 to $59,999", "11" = "$60,000 to $74,999", "12" = "$75,000 to $99,999",
  "13" = "$100,000 to $124,999", "14" = "$125,000 to $149,999", "15" = "$150,000 to $199,999", "16" = "$200,000 or more")

# Get your own
# census_api_key("your_api_key_here", install = TRUE)

df <- df %>%
  mutate(ses_thi = income_labels[as.character(ses_thi)])

# === Pull Gini index by ZIP ===
gini_zip <- get_acs(
  geography = "zcta",
  variables = c(gini = "B19083_001"),
  year = 2021,
  survey = "acs5") %>%
  dplyr::select(GEOID, estimate) %>%
  rename(zipcode = GEOID, gini = estimate)

# === Join Gini data ===
df <- df %>%
  mutate(zipcode = as.character(demo_zip_prim)) %>%
  left_join(gini_zip, by = "zipcode")

```


```{r}

df$prob_gam <- factor(df$prob_gam, levels = c(
  "no", 
  "yes"
))

summary_data <- df %>%
  group_by(prob_gam) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_rew = mean(rew_composite, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_rew = sd(rew_composite, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(rew_composite)))
  )

plot_trs <- ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = prob_gam, y = mean_rew, fill = prob_gam)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_rew - se_rew, ymax = mean_rew + se_rew), width = 0.2, size = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "",  # Remove title if you want it blank
    x = "",
    y = "Reward Sensitivtiy",
    fill = "Problem Gambling"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("yes" = "#4D94C7", "no" = "#FFD700"),
    labels = c("no" = "Non-PG", "yes" = "PG")
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("no" = "Non-PG", "yes" = "PG")) +
  theme_gray() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 24) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20, color = "black"),
  )

print(plot_trs)

# Run and print t-test results
t_test_result <- t.test(rew_composite ~ prob_gam, data = df)
print(t_test_result)

```


```{r}

summary_data <- df %>%
  group_by(prob_gam) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_yrs = mean(demo_yrs, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_yrs = sd(demo_yrs, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(demo_yrs)))
  )

plot_yrs <- ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = prob_gam, y = mean_yrs, fill = prob_gam)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_yrs - se_yrs, ymax = mean_yrs + se_yrs), width = 0.2, size = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "",  # Remove title if you want it blank
    x = "",
    y = "Age",
    fill = "Problem Gambling"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("yes" = "#4D94C7", "no" = "#FFD700"),
    labels = c("no" = "Non-PG", "yes" = "PG")
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("no" = "Non-PG", "yes" = "PG")) +
  theme_gray() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 24) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20, color = "black")
  )

print(plot_yrs)

# Run and print t-test results
t_test_result <- t.test(demo_yrs ~ prob_gam, data = df)
print(t_test_result)


```


```{r}

summary_data <- df %>%
  group_by(prob_gam) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_gini = mean(gini, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_gini = sd(gini, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(gini)))
  )

plot_gini <- ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = prob_gam, y = mean_gini, fill = prob_gam)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_gini - se_gini, ymax = mean_gini + se_gini), width = 0.2, size = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "",  # Remove title if you want it blank
    x = "",
    y = "Income Inequality",
    fill = "Problem Gambling"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("yes" = "#4D94C7", "no" = "#FFD700"),
    labels = c("no" = "Non-PG", "yes" = "PG")
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("no" = "Non-PG", "yes" = "PG")) +
  theme_gray() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 24) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "bottom"  
  )

print(plot_gini)

# Run and print t-test results
t_test_result <- t.test(gini ~ prob_gam, data = df)
print(t_test_result)


```

```{r}

df$dospert_all <- ((df$dospert_ethic + df$dospert_gam + df$dospert_hs + df$dospert_inv + df$dospert_rec + df$dospert_soc) / 40)

summary_data <- df %>%
  group_by(prob_gam) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_dospert_all = mean(dospert_all, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_dospert_all = sd(dospert_all, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(dospert_all)))
  )

plot_dospert_all <- ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = prob_gam, y = mean_dospert_all, fill = prob_gam)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_dospert_all - se_dospert_all, ymax = mean_dospert_all + se_dospert_all), width = 0.2, size = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "",  # Remove title if you want it blank
    x = "",
    y = "Risk-Taking",
    fill = "Problem Gambling"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("yes" = "navyblue", "no" = "seagreen"),
    labels = c("no" = "Non-PG", "yes" = "PG")
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("no" = "Non-PG", "yes" = "PG")) +
  theme_gray() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 24) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

print(plot_dospert_all)

```

```{r}

df$dospert_other <- ((df$dospert_ethic + df$dospert_hs + df$dospert_rec + df$dospert_soc) / 32)

summary_data <- df %>%
  group_by(prob_gam) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_dospert_other = mean(dospert_other, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_dospert_other = sd(dospert_other, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(dospert_other)))
  )

plot_dospert_other <- ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = prob_gam, y = mean_dospert_other, fill = prob_gam)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, color = "black", size = .5) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_dospert_other - se_dospert_other, ymax = mean_dospert_other + se_dospert_other), width = 0.2, size = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "",  # Remove title if you want it blank
    x = "",
    y = "Non-Financial Risk-Taking",
    fill = "Problem Gambling"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("yes" = "#4D94C7", "no" = "#FFD700"),
    labels = c("no" = "Non-PG", "yes" = "PG")
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("no" = "Non-PG", "yes" = "PG")) +
  theme_gray() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 24) +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "bottom"  
  )

print(plot_dospert_other)

# Run and print t-test results
t_test_result <- t.test(dospert_other ~ prob_gam, data = df)
print(t_test_result)

```


```{r}
ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_trs.svg", plot_trs, width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_yrs.svg", plot_yrs, width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_gini.svg", plot_gini, width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_dospert_all.svg", plot_dospert_all, width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_dospert_other.svg", plot_dospert_other, width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}

df$gsas_mental <- df$gsas_thought + df$gsas_urge

median_uclal <- median(df$uclal_total, na.rm = TRUE)

df$uclal_group <- ifelse(df$uclal_total > median_uclal, "High", "Low")
df$uclal_group <- factor(df$uclal_group, levels = c("Low", "High"))


# Create df_pg 
df_pg <- subset(df, prob_gam == "yes")

```


```{r}

# Calculate Pearson correlation
cor_test <- cor.test(df_pg$rew_composite, df_pg$gsas_total)

# Extract values
n_value <- nrow(df_pg)
r_value <- round(cor_test$estimate, 2)

# Format p-value
if (cor_test$p.value < 0.001) {
  p_value_text <- "< .001"
} else {
  p_value_text <- paste("= ", signif(cor_test$p.value, 2))
}

# Updated plot
plot_pg_rew_composite <- ggplot(df_pg, aes(x = rew_composite, y = gsas_total)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.2, size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, size = 1.5, color = "#1C1C1C", fill = "#FF4F9B", alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(
    x = "Reward Sensitivity",
    y = "Gambling Symptom\nSeverity (G-SAS)",
    title = ""
  ) +

  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold")
  )

# Print the plot
print(plot_pg_rew_composite)

print(n_value)
print(p_value_text)
print(r_value)

# Fit linear model for the plot (Gini predicting gambling symptoms)
model_rew_composite <- lm(gsas_total ~ rew_composite + demo_yrs + demo_gender + gini, data = df_pg)
summary(model_rew_composite)

```

```{r}

# Calculate Pearson correlation
cor_test <- cor.test(df_pg$susd_depression, df_pg$gsas_total)

# Extract values
n_value <- nrow(df_pg)
r_value <- round(cor_test$estimate, 2)

# Format p-value
if (cor_test$p.value < 0.001) {
  p_value_text <- "< .001"
} else {
  p_value_text <- paste("= ", signif(cor_test$p.value, 2))
}

# Updated plot
plot_pg_depression <- ggplot(df_pg, aes(x = susd_depression, y = gsas_total)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.2, size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, size = 1.5, color = "#1C1C1C", fill = "#2F5E59", alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(
    x = "Depressive Symptoms",
    y = "Gambling Symptom\nSeverity (G-SAS)",
    title = ""
  ) +

  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold")
  )

# Print the plot
print(plot_pg_depression)

print(n_value)
print(p_value_text)
print(r_value)

# Fit linear model for the plot (Gini predicting gambling symptoms)
model_depression <- lm(gsas_total ~ susd_depression + demo_yrs + demo_gender + gini, data = df_pg)
summary(model_depression)

```

```{r}

# Calculate Pearson correlation
cor_test <- cor.test(df_pg$susd_mania, df_pg$gsas_total)

# Extract values
n_value <- nrow(df_pg)
r_value <- round(cor_test$estimate, 2)

# Format p-value
if (cor_test$p.value < 0.001) {
  p_value_text <- "< .001"
} else {
  p_value_text <- paste("= ", signif(cor_test$p.value, 2))
}

# Updated plot
plot_pg_mania <- ggplot(df_pg, aes(x = susd_mania, y = gsas_total)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.2, size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, size = 1.5, color = "#1C1C1C", fill = "#D72638", alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(
    x = "Mania Symptoms",
    y = "Gambling Symptom\nSeverity (G-SAS)",
    title = ""
  ) +

  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold")
  )

# Print the plot
print(plot_pg_mania)

print(n_value)
print(p_value_text)
print(r_value)

# Fit linear model for the plot (Gini predicting gambling symptoms)
model_mania <- lm(gsas_total ~ susd_mania + demo_yrs + demo_gender + gini, data = df_pg)
summary(model_mania)

```

```{r}

# Calculate Pearson correlation
cor_test <- cor.test(df_pg$dospert_other, df_pg$gsas_total)

# Extract values
n_value <- nrow(df_pg)
r_value <- round(cor_test$estimate, 2)

# Format p-value
if (cor_test$p.value < 0.001) {
  p_value_text <- "< .001"
} else {
  p_value_text <- paste("= ", signif(cor_test$p.value, 2))
}

# Updated plot
plot_pg_dospert_other <- ggplot(df_pg, aes(x = dospert_other, y = gsas_total)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.3, size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, size = 1.5, color = "#1C1C1C", fill = "#CBA135", alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(
    x = "Non-Financial Risk-Taking",
    y = "Gambling Symptom\nSeverity (G-SAS)",
    title = ""
  ) +

  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold")
  )

# Print the plot
print(plot_pg_dospert_other)

print(n_value)
print(p_value_text)
print(r_value)

# Fit linear model for the plot (Gini predicting gambling symptoms)
model_dospert_other <- lm(formula = gsas_total ~ rew_composite * demo_yrs * gini + dospert_other + demo_gender, data = df_pg)
summary(model_dospert_other)

```

```{r}

# Plot with uclal_group as the moderator and matching SE fill colors
plot_pg_uclal <- ggplot(df_pg, aes(x = rew_composite, y = gsas_mental, color = uclal_group, fill = uclal_group)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.3, size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, size = 1.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(
    x = "Reward Sensitivity",
    y = "Thoughts & Urges\n(G-SAS)",
    title = ""
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Low" = "#009C8A", "High" = "#E76B3C")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Low" = "#009C8A", "High" = "#E76B3C")
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

# Print the plot
print(plot_pg_uclal)

# Fit linear model for the plot (Gini predicting gambling symptoms)
model_lone <- lm(formula = gsas_mental ~ rew_composite * demo_yrs * uclal_total, data = df_pg)
summary(model_lone)

```

```{r}

# Install grid package
# install.packages("gridExtra")

# Load necessary library
library(gridExtra)

# Example: arrange 4 plots (replace these with your actual plots)
plot_one_grid <- grid.arrange(
  plot_race, plot_gender,
  plot_density_simple, plot_gam, 
  ncol = 2, nrow = 2
)


```


```{r}
# Set global width and height for saved plots
plot_width <- 5
plot_height <- 5
plot_dpi <- 300

# Save all plots with consistent dimensions
ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_pg_rew_composite.svg", 
       plot_pg_rew_composite, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, dpi = plot_dpi)

ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_one_grid.svg", 
       plot_one_grid, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, dpi = plot_dpi)

ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_pg_depression.svg", 
       plot_pg_depression, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, dpi = plot_dpi)

ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_pg_mania.svg", 
       plot_pg_mania, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, dpi = plot_dpi)

ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_pg_uclal.svg", 
       plot_pg_uclal, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, dpi = plot_dpi)

ggsave("../../derivatives/plotting/plot_pg_dospert_other.svg", 
       plot_pg_dospert_other, width = plot_width, height = plot_height, dpi = plot_dpi)

```
